sections.paragraph:
ἀνταποκρίνομαι 1 aor. mid. 3 sg. ἀνταπεκρίνατο (Just.); pass. ἀνταπεκρίθην (s. ἀποκρίνομαι; mathematical t.t.=correspond to [Nicomachus Gerasenus, Arithmet. 1, 8, 10f]; LXX; TestJob 5:1; 41:1; Just. A I, 17, 2 [Mt 22:21 par.]) answer in turn (Aesop 301a, 6 [Ch.]; schol. on Pind., P. 9, 65; Syntipas p. 80, 12; Leontios 35 p. 68, 23 τινί; Judg 5:29 A; Job; TestJob 5:1 al.) οὐκ ἴσχυσαν ἀ. πρὸς ταῦτα they could make no reply to this Lk 14:6 (cp. Job 32:12). ὁ ἀνταποκρινόμενος τῷ θεῷ one who answers back to God Ro 9:20 (cp. Job 16:8; Pind., P. 2, 88 χρὴ δὲ πρὸς θεὸν οὐκ ἐρίζειν=one must not contend against God).—DELG s.v. κρίνω. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Nicomachus Nicomachus Gerasenus , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

par. par. = parallel

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 87
